article_title: Rust 1.95 Adds `cfg_select!`, Extends `if let` Guards, and Tightens Stable Target Handling

content_preview.title: Rust 1.95 Adds `cfg_select!`, Extends `if let` Guards, and Tightens Stable Target Handling
content_preview.summary: Rust 1.95 lands with a new `cfg_select!` macro, `if let` guards in `match`, stabilized APIs, and a stricter stance on custom target JSON on stable.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Rust 1.95 shipped on April 16, 2026, adding a new `cfg_select!` macro, extending `if let` guards into `match` expressions, and stabilizing a long list of APIs, according to [the Rust release announcement](https://blog.rust-lang.org/2026/04/16/Rust-1.95.0/). This follows [our March coverage of Rust 1.94](/article/2026-03/22-rust-194-ships-array-windows-and-avx-512-fp16-as-65-project-goals-chart-the-languages-2026-roadmap) and shows the language keeping up a rapid release cadence.

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear and concise release coverage with appropriate technical depth for a language-update news article.
editor_notes.source_verification: Fetched and read all 4 cited sources. The Rust Blog directly supports the core release claims about cfg_select!, if-let guards in match, stabilized APIs, const-context stabilizations, and stable custom target JSON support removal. Rust Internals confirms the April 13 pre-release testing post and April 16 release schedule. Phoronix's release recap supports the broader summary, and its Linux 7.0 preparation piece supports the downstream kernel-compatibility claim.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: The main release details and timeline align with the cited sources. No fabricated quotes or clear misattributions found. The kernel-preparation paragraph is supported by the cited Phoronix Linux 7.0 article.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, professional, and appropriately technical throughout.
editor_notes.originality: Not a duplicate. Existing archive coverage mentions Rust 1.95 only in passing from earlier Rust 1.94 and Linux 7.0 articles; this is distinct coverage of the 1.95 release itself.
recommendations: Consider adding Rust Blog, Rust Internals, and Phoronix to the source allowlist if they are expected to recur in programming-language coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Approved. This is a source-grounded, original release article with accurate attribution and sufficient context.
